pages.raw_text:
Engine No. 100 was frisky colt
and did not take kindly to the narrow
confines Of two steel bands. but had
a penchant for the green and fertile
fields Of the Palouse country. At least
so it would seem from the complaint
in the case of Edward L. Keysef
against the Northern Pacific company.
in which Keyser asks $4,000 for in
juries received while riding In the en-
CINE.


The complaint says the engine had
been out OF order for some time be-
fore the accident, which occurred on
October % last; that the engine had
repeatedly run of the track and that
this was known to the superintendent
and to the master mechanic.
